# Vendor: MetaHarness (agent-harness-generator)

Vendored snapshot of the **deterministic recommendation data** from:

- **npm**: `metaharness@0.1.5` (the CLI; `@ruvnet/agent-harness-generator@0.1.3` is a
  re-export wrapper around it)
- **source**: https://github.com/ruvnet/agent-harness-generator
- **license**: MIT (see `LICENSE`)

## What is vendored

ONLY the shipped template catalog (`templates/catalog.json`), reduced to a
compact recommendation index (`catalog-index.json`):

```
{ id, domain, name, desc, tags, agents: string[], skills: string[], mcps: string[] }
```

The CLI bins, interactive `prompts`, render/walker/manifest machinery, and the
full template file-trees are NOT vendored. The CortexOS agent generator does
NOT scaffold a foreign harness — it drives the existing Hermes pipeline
(`scripts/hermes-profile-create.mjs` + systemd units). MetaHarness is used
solely to SEED the `skills` / `agents` suggestion lists the AI then refines.

## Provenance

The index was generated from the published tarball:

```
npm pack metaharness@0.1.5   →   templates/catalog.json   →   catalog-index.json
```

`agentCount`/`skillCount` in the upstream catalog are summary counts; the
vendored `agents`/`skills` arrays carry the concrete NAME lists. `mcpServers`
entries that are template placeholders (`{{name}}`) are dropped; only concrete
server names are kept.

## Why vendor (not `npm install`)

Per the project decision, profile creation must not depend on a third-party
runtime. Vendoring the static catalog data keeps the generator self-contained
and auditable; the upstream package is not a runtime dependency of any
service.
